task: Harden the Blue Life Commons production first viewport with one real licensed, review-visible species record.
surface: Responsive public home page first viewport
audience: Public learners, citizen scientists, researchers, conservation organizations, educators, and open-data reusers
first_read: Blue Life Commons publishes source-linked, review-gated ocean-life records with visible rights and Git history.

references.label: Whale shark artifact
references.reason: Real record, sources, review state, rights, approved media, and reuse license

references.label: Public media guard
references.reason: Confirms approved public image and blocks candidate media

references.label: Blue Life brand pack
references.reason: Existing oceanic editorial tokens and brand posture

avoid: Implied scientific approval or hidden review state
direction.signature: One whale shark record exposes its source, rights, review, reuse, and Git history in the first viewport.
system: Tier A approved record and CC0 media
One source-trace motion with static reduced route
Visible media credit and artifact license

asset_quality.why_premium: The first viewport uses a real governed species record and its approved licensed media rather than decorative imagery.

artifacts.notes: Implementation complete; desktop/mobile export inspection pending.
Existing record inspected: status, sources, media rights, review checks, and reuse license are explicit.

checks.name: source-and-rights
checks.evidence: Whale shark record declares three sources; media is approved CC0 with 9/9 checks.

checks.name: review-honesty
checks.evidence: The surface labels scientific review as pending and media as approved.

checks.name: export-inspection
checks.evidence: The single bounded Edge capture attempt detached before page load and produced a connection-refused artifact. The invalid artifact was removed and the capture was not retried.

checks.name: visual-qa
checks.evidence: Live preview and responsive inspection remain.

score.notes: Specific, honest Tier A proof is implemented. Export and preview inspection are required before ship.

known_risks: The remote Wikimedia image depends on source availability unless the approved Vercel Blob mirror is present.
The underlying species record still requires expert review; the UI must never imply otherwise.
Custom domain and production state remain out of scope.
Desktop and mobile visual inspection remain blocked after the capped capture attempt.
